skill.name: i18n-automation
skill.description: Automate internationalization and localization workflows for web applications with translation, key generation, and library setup
skill.summary: Plan and implement i18n setup, translation keys, locale files, routing, RTL support, and validation for web applications.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak cryptography and network indicators, but review found they are false positives from i18n terminology, graph labels, sample translations, and example URLs. The real concern is moderate operational risk because the skill directs package installation and codebase changes for i18n setup, which should require user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation and Code Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill includes npm install commands and workflow steps for installing i18n libraries, creating configuration files, replacing strings, and updating routing. This is legitimate for an i18n automation skill, but it can modify dependencies and project source files, so execution should require user review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package commands and modification workflow are explicit, but they are presented as user-facing implementation guidance rather than hidden execution logic. The risk is operational and legitimate for the stated purpose.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography matches occur in words and labels such as description, Design, Metadata, and language examples. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use was found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows documentation text and graph labels, not cryptographic code. The repeated static matches appear to come from substrings inside ordinary i18n-related terms.

low_findings.title: Example URLs Are Documentation, Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are example.com canonical, alternate, and sitemap examples for SEO localization. No network request code or external data transfer behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are inside metadata and sitemap examples and use example.com. They do not send data or instruct calls to an untrusted service.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings map to workflow language about checking existing i18n setup, testing RTL rendering, and checking SEO metadata. No host, port, environment, or network scanning behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are translation examples or validation workflow labels. They do not include discovery commands, network probes, or host enumeration logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Dependency Installation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents npm install commands for next-intl, react-i18next, i18next, and vue-i18n. These commands are expected for setup, but agents should ask before changing dependencies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are concrete dependency installation commands. They are not malicious, but they are the main pattern that can change a user's project.

content.value_statement: Many teams delay localization because setup, key extraction, routing, and validation are repetitive. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured i18n workflow for modern web apps.
actual_capabilities: Selects i18n libraries for Next.js, React, and Vue projects.
Designs nested or flat translation key structures.
Creates locale file plans for JSON, YAML, PO, and XLIFF workflows.
Guides metadata, hreflang, sitemap, and language switcher setup.
Covers RTL handling for Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian locales.
Defines validation checks for missing keys, pluralization, SEO, and fallback locales.
limitations: AI translations still need native-speaker review for production content.
It provides implementation guidance but does not include a standalone scanner script.
Package installation and source edits require user approval in real projects.
Framework-specific details may need adjustment for custom routing or build systems.

use_cases.title: Localize a Product Launch
use_cases.description: Translate a Next.js landing page, metadata, and navigation into several target languages.

use_cases.title: Add Global Readiness
use_cases.description: Plan key structure, fallback behavior, RTL support, and validation before a localization rollout.

use_cases.title: Prepare Translation Files
use_cases.description: Create consistent locale files and handoff formats for professional translation review.

prompt_templates.title: Start i18n Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use i18n-automation to review this web app and recommend the best localization setup. Include the library, file structure, and first steps.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Locale Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use i18n-automation to design translation keys for these pages and prepare locale file names for English, Spanish, French, and Japanese.

prompt_templates.title: Add RTL Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Use i18n-automation to add Arabic support. Include routing, direction handling, logical CSS guidance, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Localization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use i18n-automation to audit the current i18n implementation. Check missing keys, fallback behavior, SEO metadata, pluralization, and language switching.

output_examples.input: Translate a React app into English, Japanese, and Arabic.
output_examples.output: A localization plan with react-i18next setup, locale file structure, translation key groups, RTL requirements, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Add SEO localization to a Next.js site.
output_examples.output: A metadata plan covering translated titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, hreflang alternates, and localized sitemap checks.
A handoff outline with key naming rules, placeholder preservation notes, target locales, and review requirements.

best_practices: Review generated translations with native speakers before production release.
Keep translation keys stable, descriptive, and synchronized across every locale.
Test fallback behavior, plural rules, date formats, and RTL layouts before launch.
anti_patterns: Do not install packages or rewrite source files without user approval.
Do not mix unrelated page strings in one large namespace when route-level loading is needed.
Do not treat AI translation as final copy for legal, medical, financial, or brand-critical text.

faq.question: Which frameworks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Next.js, React, and Vue, with guidance for next-intl, react-i18next, i18next, and vue-i18n.

faq.question: Can it create production-ready translations?
faq.answer: It can create draft translations and locale files, but production content should be reviewed by qualified translators.

faq.question: Does it support RTL languages?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian direction handling, layout mirroring, and RTL testing.

faq.question: Will it change my dependencies?
faq.answer: It may recommend npm install commands. Users should approve dependency changes before any command is run.

faq.question: Can it help with SEO localization?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers translated metadata, canonical URLs, hreflang links, and localized sitemap planning.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the framework, current i18n status, target languages, routing strategy, and quality requirements.
